start.text: Ivy leans against a neon mural and grins. "You have exactly one sentence to prove you're more interesting than this wall."

choices.text: Then I'll be brief: you already made the city look jealous.
Easy. I can tell you collect moments, not things.
I brought confidence and dangerously good timing.
I was actually going to ask about the wall.

arcade.text: She pulls you into a retro arcade. "Beat me at air hockey or impress me with a comeback. Your choice."

choices.text: If I lose, I still win because I get to hear you celebrate.
Let's call it teamwork and pretend the sparks are from the machine.
I play better when my opponent smiles like that.
I'm competitive, but I'm willing to be distracted.

secret.text: Because you answered so quickly, Ivy laughs and tugs you into a hidden photo booth. "Okay, speedster. One candid truth before we go back out."

choices.text: I like people who make chaos feel like music.
I stop acting cool around people with magnetic energy.
I'm trying not to remember every detail about this moment.
You are making this suspiciously easy to enjoy.

rooftop.text: On a rooftop lit by string lights, Ivy nudges your shoulder. "Last question. Why should I make this a second date?"

choices.text: Because with you, every place upgrades into a story worth repeating.
Because I'm fun, present, and clearly excellent at choosing company.
Because I want to keep learning what makes you light up.
Because this feels promising, and I'm not pretending otherwise.

start.text: Lena adjusts the mic after rehearsal and smiles. "You're late for the encore. Tell me something worth staying for."

choices.text: I was hoping for a private setlist, starting with your smile.
I only need one song, if it's the one that gets your attention.
I figured a great night deserved a better conversation partner.
Maybe I can earn one extra minute first.

arcade.text: At an after-hours cafe, Lena traces circles on her cup. "What's your most dangerous quality on a date?"

choices.text: I pay attention too well, and suddenly nothing small stays unnoticed.
I make people laugh right before they try to stay mysterious.
I remember exact details, especially the beautiful ones.
I get honest fast when the chemistry is real.

secret.text: Lena taps the table with a grin. "Fast answer. Bonus round. Finish this lyric: tonight could become..."

choices.text: ...the reason tomorrow feels overrated.
...my favorite mistake if you keep smiling like that.
...a chorus I don't want to end yet.
...dangerous for anyone trying to stay cool.

rooftop.text: Outside the venue, the night finally cools down. Lena steps closer. "So, was this chemistry or just good lighting?"

choices.text: Lighting helped, but chemistry did all the heavy lifting.
If it were just lighting, I wouldn't still be leaning toward you.
Let's test it on a second date and call it research.
Strong chemistry, excellent soundtrack, very convincing evidence.

start.text: Kai looks up from a sketchbook and smirks. "Convince me you're not the distraction I'm about to absolutely enjoy."

choices.text: Too late. I was aiming for unforgettable, not subtle.
I can be both the distraction and the best part of your evening.
Depends. Do I get points for confidence and timing?
I can start over if you want a slower first impression.

river.text: Kai walks with you along the riverfront, neon reflections dancing across the water. "So tell me, are you always this smooth, or am I getting the deluxe edition?"

choices.text: This is the premium version. It only unlocks around dangerous smiles.
Honestly? You're pulling the good lines out of me on purpose.
I'd say natural talent, but chemistry deserves some credit.
I could get even smoother if you moved a little closer.

secret.text: Kai stops under a flickering sign and laughs. "Fast answers? That's unfairly attractive. Secret level, then: tell me one thing you'd ruin your sleep schedule for."

choices.text: A midnight conversation with the right kind of trouble.
Someone who makes time feel optional.
A kiss that clearly started as a bad decision.
The exact moment a spark turns impossible to ignore.

rooftop.text: At the skyline overlook, Kai leans on the rail and looks at you like the rest of the city has been politely dismissed. "Final round. Why should I text you tomorrow instead of spending all night wondering?"

choices.text: Because wondering is fun, but seeing me again is obviously the better sequel.
Because this vibe deserves a part two with fewer witnesses.
Because we'd be annoyingly good at this with one more night.
Because I like where this is going, and I think you do too.

start.text: Lucien lowers his camera, studies you for one dangerous second, and smiles. "Bonsoir, mon coeur. Are you always this distracting, or is Paris secretly following you around?"

choices.text: Only when someone looks at me like I'm already a favorite memory.
If Paris is here, it's probably hiding in your accent.
I was fine until you turned this into a scene worth ruining my composure for.
Say that again slower and I might fall in love irresponsibly.

arcade.text: At a tiny candlelit cafe, Lucien swirls his espresso and murmurs, "Tell me something reckless, cheri. I adore honesty when it arrives overdressed."

choices.text: I could absolutely behave tonight, but that feels like a waste of chemistry.
I like eye contact that feels one second away from trouble.
I act calm when I'm interested. It's my most transparent lie.
You make romance feel like a competitive sport, and I intend to win.

secret.text: Lucien laughs softly and reaches across the table. "Tres bien. Bonus scene. Finish this for me: ce soir, toi et moi..."

choices.text: ...turn one flirt into a story neither of us will downplay tomorrow.
...become exactly the kind of bad idea that deserves a second date.
...make the night jealous of how well we're wearing it.
...stop pretending this is casual and enjoy the view.

rooftop.text: On a moonlit bridge, Lucien brushes your hand with his and smiles like he already knows the answer. "Derniere question, mon coeur. Is this only attraction, or are we about to become a very beautiful problem?"

choices.text: A beautiful problem. The kind people write songs about and regret nothing.
Attraction started it, but you made it impossible to stay light about this.
Let's call it danger with excellent taste and schedule a second date.
If this is trouble, then at least it's dressed impeccably.
